instructionSummary: The baby scratch is the base movement for scratch control: one clean push forward and one clean pull back with the fader left open.
coachScript: Keep the record hand loose. Match the length and weight of the forward and backward sound so the scratch feels balanced before you add speed.
steps: Set the fader open and keep it there.
Push the record forward with one smooth sound.
Pull the record back with the same distance and pressure.
Repeat slowly until both directions sound even.
commonMistake: Driving the forward motion harder than the pullback so the scratch loses balance.
practiceChallenge: Land 8 steady baby scratches in a row without changing the volume or the pace.
